steps.title: Identify the decay factor
steps.content: The mass decreases by 23% every 11.69 minutes, so 77% remains each period. The decay factor is 0.77.

steps.title: Build the exponent
steps.content: For exponential decay with period $T$, the model is $M = M_0 \cdot b^{\frac{t}{T}}$. Here $b = 0.77$ and $T = 11.69$, giving $M = 100(0.77)^{\frac{t}{11.69}}$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $M=100(0.77)^{\frac{t}{11.69}}$: base 0.77 (77% remains) and exponent $\frac{t}{11.69}$ (number of periods). That is the correct answer.
  • Choice B — $M=100(0.77)^{t+11.69}$: exponent $t+11.69$ is not the number of periods elapsed. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — $M=100(0.23)^{\frac{t}{11.69}}$: base 0.23 would mean only 23% remains (the amount lost, not retained). Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $M=100(0.23)^{t+11.69}$: wrong base and wrong exponent form. Eliminated.
